trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void emitWindowContents(\n            W window, Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState)\n            throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // Work around type system restrictions...\n        FluentIterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> recordsWithTimestamp =\n                FluentIterable.from(contents)\n                        .transform(\n                                new Function<StreamRecord<IN>, TimestampedValue<IN>>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public TimestampedValue<IN> apply(StreamRecord<IN> input) {\n                                        return TimestampedValue.from(input);\n                                    }\n                                });\n        evictorContext.evictBefore(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        FluentIterable<IN> projectedContents =\n                recordsWithTimestamp.transform(\n                        new Function<TimestampedValue<IN>, IN>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public IN apply(TimestampedValue<IN> input) {\n                                return input.getValue();\n                            }\n                        });\n\n        processContext.window = triggerContext.window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key,\n                triggerContext.window,\n                processContext,\n                projectedContents,\n                timestampedCollector);\n        evictorContext.evictAfter(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        // work around to fix FLINK-4369, remove the evicted elements from the windowState.\n        // this is inefficient, but there is no other way to remove elements from ListState, which\n        // is an AppendingState.\n        windowState.update(\n                recordsWithTimestamp.stream()\n                        .map(TimestampedValue::getStreamRecord)\n                        .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n    }\n\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code EvictorContext} is a utility for handling {@code Evictor} invocations. It can be\n     * reused by setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept\n     * in the {@code EvictorContext}.\n     */\n    class EvictorContext implements Evictor.EvictorContext {\n\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public EvictorContext(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return EvictingWindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public K getKey() {\n            return key;\n        }\n\n        void evictBefore(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictBefore((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n\n        void evictAfter(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictAfter((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        evictorContext = new EvictorContext(null, null);\n        evictingWindowState =\n                (InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>>)\n                        getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, evictingWindowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        evictorContext = null;\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Evictor<? {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_015Qzx5Kz5RGiAUsAsNjxrGv","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Great! Now I've applied the fix to both WindowOperator and EvictingWindowOperator. The fix is to remove the `isSkippedElement &&` condition, so that late elements are always side output if they have a tag configured.\n\nHowever, I realize there might be an issue with this fix. Let me think about it:\n\nThe original logic was:\n- If the element is NOT processed (isSkippedElement = true) AND it's late, then side output it\n\nThe new logic is:\n- If the element is late, then side output it (regardless of whether it was processed)\n\nThis change means that:\n1. Late elements that are merged into existing windows will now be side output\n2. Late elements that are not merged will also be side output (as before)\n\nBut there's a potential issue: what if we're double-emitting the late data? Once to the normal output (as part of the window processing) and once to the side output?\n\nLooking at the test case I created, the expectation is that:\n1. The late element triggers window output (because of the merge)\n2. The late element is also side output\n\nSo double-emission is expected and desired. This makes sense because:\n- The main output contains the result of processing the window with the late element included\n- The side output contains the late element itself, for tracking/monitoring purposes\n\nSo the fix is correct. Let me now verify that the test I added is correct and that my understanding of the fix is correct.\n\nActually, looking at my test more carefully:\n- Element at 5000 creates session [5000, 5003]\n- Element at 4500 (which is late) merges with the session to create [4500, 5003]\n- The merged session fires and outputs a tuple with key \"key1-2\" (sum of 1+1=2) and window [4500, 5003]\n- The late element 4500 is also side output\n\nSo yes, the element appears in both outputs. This is the expected behavior.\n\n\nLet me trace through the test execution to confirm the expectations. The element at 5000 creates window [5000, 5003], then when the late element arrives at 4500, it gets assigned to [4500, 4503] and merges with the existing window to form [4500, 5003]. This merged window fires and produces output, while the late element is simultaneously side output.\n\nWhen the watermark reaches 8000, the EventTimeTrigger condition (watermark >= window.maxTimestamp() + 1) is satisfied since 8000 >= 5004, so the trigger should have already fired. I need to verify the exact timing of when the trigger fires relative to the watermark advancement and element processing. 000, 5003] was already fired and purged. But EventTimeTrigger doesn't automatically purge window state, so the window data persists. Let me trace through the sequence more carefully—the element at 4500 arrives after the watermark reaches 8000, which means the window [5000, 5003] has already fired. When the late-arriving element tries to merge with it, I need to verify whether the window was cleaned up or if the merge still occurs with existing state.\n\nThe key question is whether the MergingWindowSet still contains [5000, 5003] when the element at 4500 is processed, or if cleanup has already removed it. I should check the WindowOperator's cleanup logic to understand when windows are actually removed from the merge set.\n\nLooking at the existing test `testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness()`, I can see the pattern: an element at 10000 arrives after the watermark has passed 5003, and it gets side-output without merging. For my test to work, I need an element that both merges with an existing window and gets side-output as late. Let me trace through what conditions would allow that to happen.\n\nThe key insight is that session windows fire when watermark >= maxTimestamp + 1. So for a window [5000, 5003], it fires when watermark reaches 5004. If I want an element to merge with this window after it's been cleaned up, I need to ensure the window has already fired and been discarded. Let me work through a sequence: start with an element at 5000 creating [5000, 5003], advance the watermark past 5004 so it fires, then send a late element that would have merged with it. But the window is already gone, so it becomes a side output instead. ges with [5000, 5003], creating [5000, 5004]. Watermark 8000 fires this window since 8000 >= 5005. An element at 4000 arriving at watermark 8000 wouldn't be late because 4000 + 1000 = 5000, which is not <= 8000.\n\nI'm struggling to construct a scenario where a late element actually merges with an unfired window. For that to happen, the element needs to satisfy the late condition (timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark) while also falling within an existing window's time range that hasn't fired yet. The constraints seem to work against each other in most cases I'm testing.\n\nLet me reconsider what's required. An element is late when element.timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark. A window hasn't fired when window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness > watermark. So I need an element where its timestamp plus allowed lateness is at or before the watermark, but the window's max timestamp plus allowed lateness is still after the watermark. Testing with element at 5000, allowedLateness 1000, and watermark 6500: the element becomes late (5000 + 1000 = 6000 <= 6500), but the window [5000, 5003] is also late (5003 + 1000 = 6003 <= 6500). Both conditions can't be satisfied simultaneously with these parameters.\n\nFor this to work, the element's timestamp must be less than or equal to the window's max timestamp. With a session window using gap 3000 and allowedLateness 2000, I can construct a scenario: an element at 5000 creates window [5000, 8000], then watermark advances to 11000. A second element at 9000 creates window [9000, 12000]. When element 8500 arrives at watermark 11000, it's late (8500 + 2000 = 10500 <= 11000) and gets assigned to window [8500, 11500], which hasn't fired yet since 11500 + 2000 = 13500 > 11000.\n\nThe key insight is that session windows merge based on overlap—[8500, 11500] overlaps with [9000, 12000], merging into [8500, 12000]. This merged window isn't late (12000 + 2000 = 14000 > 11000), so the element processes normally rather than being side-output. I need to verify when windows actually get removed from the MergingWindowSet—either through firing with purging triggers or explicit cleanup timers.\n\nSince EventTimeTrigger doesn't purge by default, the window [5000, 8000] persists in the set even after firing at watermark 11000. This means the merging behavior should work as expected. Rather than overcomplicating the test with these edge cases, I should follow the simpler patterns in the existing test suite and use straightforward numbers that clearly demonstrate the late element merging scenario.\n\nLooking at the test examples, most don't actually cover the case where a late-arriving element merges with a window that's already been created. I need to construct a scenario with smaller gaps and lateness windows. Using Gap = 3 and allowedLateness = 100, I can set up: an element at 1000 creates [1000, 1003], then at 1200 another element creates [1200, 1203] which merges into [1000, 1203]. After the watermark advances to 1204 and fires this window, a late element arriving at 1100 gets assigned to [1100, 1103] but can't merge with the already-fired [1000, 1203] window since it's been cleaned up. The issue is that the existing window fires before the late element can merge with it. I should either use a trigger that doesn't automatically fire, or switch to a processing-time based trigger to avoid this timing problem. this window was already fired. Does it still exist?\n\nIn the existing code, when a window fires, it's not immediately removed from the MergingWindowSet. It's only removed when the cleanup timer fires at window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness + 1. For window [1000, 1203], that's 1203 + 100 + 1 = 1304, so at watermark 1204 the window still exists and the element at 1100 would merge with it without creating a new window. The test should work with adjusted numbers to match the simpler scenario.\n\nBut there's a critical issue: when a late element merges into a window that already fired, the trigger fires again. Since the trigger's onElement method gets called when an element is added to the window, and the EventTimeTrigger condition is already satisfied (watermark 1204 > maxTimestamp 1203 + 1), the window contents get emitted a second time, causing duplicate output.\n\nThe expected behavior should be: first output the sum of 2 from the initial window firing, then output sum of 3 when the late element arrives and triggers the window again. The late element itself should also appear in the side output. I should verify this understanding against the existing test cases to see how they handle late-arriving elements.\n\nLooking at `testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLatenessPurgingTrigger()`, I notice it side-outputs late elements at 10000 and 10100, then processes a later element at 14500. The key difference is that test uses a purging trigger, which clears the window after firing, preventing late elements from merging back in. With EventTimeTrigger instead, which doesn't purge, late arrivals can actually combine with the already-fired window and trigger it again.\n\nThis means my test scenario should work as intended. I'll simplify the numbers and make the expectations explicit.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":494,"cache_read_input_tokens":64663,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":494},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"db65991f-102a-4f08-ab76-6b64c847f19b","uuid":"d98679fd-511d-4bad-bb9f-8122933dce38"}

harnessProvider.create();\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n     stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n                   operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testContinuousWatermarkTrigger(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        GlobalWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                new GlobalWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, GlobalWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                ContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, GlobalWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                null, /*Required async trigger*/\n                                new ExecutionConfig(),\n testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // The global window actually ignores these timestamps...\n\n testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n               expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4000));\n testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(8000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n Tuple3<>(\"key2-21\", 13000L, 16000L), 15999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name     // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n               new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        // Test that late-arriving elements that merge into existing sessions\n        // are still emitted to the side output when sideOutputLateData is configured\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 1000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Create first session [5000, 5003]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 5000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4999));\n\n        // Move watermark forward\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(8000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(8000));\n\n        // Now send a late element (4500 + 1000 = 5500 <= 8000, so it's late)\n        // This element will merge with the existing session [5000, 5003]\n        // creating a new session [4500, 5003]\n        // Even though the element merges, it should still be side output because it's late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // The late element should appear in the side output\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // The merged session should fire and emit\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-2\", 4500L, 5003L), 5002));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction2()))\n                        : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction2\n            implements WindowFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<String> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            out.collect(\"GOT: \" + Joiner.on(\",\").join(input));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new                 String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) of the fired\n     * window. This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {\n            ctx.deleteEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean canMerge() {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n      return \"EventTimeTrigger()\";\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger\n            extends Trigger<Tuple2<String, Integer>, TimeWindow> {\n The original condition only side outputs when no windows processed the element AND it's late, which works for non-merging windows but fails for merging ones—a late element can merge into an active window and get processed without being side output, even though it arrived late.\n\nMy fix removes the isSkippedElement check entirely, ensuring all late elements are side output regardless of processing status. Checking the implications: elements that previously weren't side output will now be, which is the intended fix. late-arriving elements that merge into existing sessions\n        // are still emitted to the side output when sideOutputLateData is configured.\n        // This test verifies the fix for the bug where late elements merging into\n        // non-late windows were not being side output.\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 1000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                     new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n 1003]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(999));\n\n        // Create another element that extends the first session to [1000, 2003]\n        // Gap between 1000 and 2000 is 1000, which is < 3000, so they merge\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));\n\n        // Window fires when watermark > maxTimestamp + 1 = 2003 + 1 = 2004\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-2\", 1000L, 2003L), 2002));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(3000));\n\n        // Now send a late element (800 + 1000 = 1800 <= 3000, so it's late)\n        // This element will be assigned to window [800, 803] and merge with [1000, 2003]\n        // to create [800, 2003]\n        // This merged window is not late (2003 + 1000 > 3000)\n        // So the element is processed for merging\n        // But it should also be side output because it's late data\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 800));\n\n        // The late element should appear in the side output\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 800));\n\n        // The merged session should fire again (since watermark > 2004)\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 800L, 2003L), 2002));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }","originalFile":"/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one\n * or more sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the ContinuousEventTimeTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithContinuousEventTimeTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order and first trigger time is 2000\n        testHarness.processElement(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // triggers emit and next trigger time is 4000\n testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 4000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n        // triggers emit and next trigger time is 6000\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 1500L, 7000L), 6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-15\", 0L, 7000L), 6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * This tests a custom Session window assigner that assigns some elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                new PointSessionWindows(3000),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            // add elements out-of-order\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n            testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-36\", 10L, 4000L), 3999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-67\", 0L, 3000L), 2999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                    \"Output was not correct.\",\n                    expectedOutput,\n                    testHarness.getOutput(),\n                    new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperatorFactory<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {\n        try {\n            return new KeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<>(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createAsyncTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperator<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // this is side output as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // this is also side output as late (we test that they are not accidentally merged)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness() throws Exception {\n     // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. The expected\n        // results are therefore slightly different.\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n
